title: Gateway: NAT64/DNS64 for IPv4 internet access
description:
## Overview

Most internet infrastructure is still IPv4. NAT64 + DNS64 lets IPv6-only mesh nodes reach IPv4 hosts.

## How it works

### DNS64
1. Mesh node queries DNS for example.com (AAAA record)
2. Gateway DNS proxy intercepts
3. If no AAAA exists, query for A record
4. Synthesize AAAA: 64:ff9b::192.0.2.1 (NAT64 prefix + IPv4)
5. Return synthesized AAAA to mesh node

### NAT64
1. Mesh node sends packet to 64:ff9b::192.0.2.1
2. Gateway recognizes NAT64 prefix
3. Extract IPv4 address, translate packet to IPv4
4. NAT the source (mesh node) to gateway's IPv4 address
5. Forward to IPv4 internet
6. Reverse for replies

## Implementation options

### Option 1: External NAT64 (recommended for RPi)
- Run Jool or TAYGA on Linux
- Gateway just forwards to NAT64 box
- More flexible, battle-tested

### Option 2: Built-in NAT64 (for embedded)
- Minimal NAT64 in gateway firmware
- Only TCP/UDP/ICMP
- Stateful NAT with connection tracking
- More complex, but self-contained

### Option 3: IPv6-only (simplest)
- Require IPv6 upstream
- No NAT64 needed
- Limits compatibility but zero complexity

## Recommendation

- RPi gateway: Option 1 (external Jool)
- Heltec/embedded: Option 3 (IPv6-only) for MVP, Option 2 later
- Document how to set up external NAT64 for mixed deployments

## Spec note

NAT64 is transparent to mesh nodes. They just see IPv6 addresses. The gateway handles translation.
close_reason: Added explicit NAT64 prefix (64:ff9b::/96) check in is_local_mesh to ensure upstream forwarding for IPv4 internet via NAT64 as described. Updated test. Commit 0524928
